title: Pending in-scope docs IPD (assess-documentation) sits in .agents/plans/pending/
impact: Shipping with a known-inaccurate README (dead config key) while a prepared fix sits pending
recommended_action: Fold the docs-IPD fixes into this review's Section 4/7

title: Version still 1.0.4 (already on PyPI) with a large [Unreleased] changelog set
evidence: ocman.py:191 __version__=1.0.4; pyproject version=1.0.4; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] present; 1.0.4 tagged/published
impact: Re-publishing without a bump would collide with the published 1.0.4 on PyPI
recommended_action: Bump version + finalize CHANGELOG at release (Section 6 plan, Section 9 execute)
validation: Section 6 decides target version; user bumps at release

title: Secrets/PII scan: 4432 candidates, all triaged as false positives
evidence: scan_secrets.py tree+history; medium hits are timestamp-strings (20260101-120000) flagged as credit-card + model IDs (its_direct/pt1-qwen3-32b-us) flagged as high-entropy; us-phone/ipv4 in example prose
impact: No real secret or PII committed; scan is clean
recommended_action: No action; keep gitleaks/detect-secrets in CI (S6)

title: ocman.py monolith continues to grow (~8.7k lines; +879 in delta)
evidence: single-module CLI by deliberate KISS design (ARCHITECTURE 'monolith is a deliberate trade-off'); delta added 4 feature areas + confirm seam
impact: Larger surface to navigate; but intentional per stated principle
recommended_action: No split now (would be a broad refactor); revisit only if a natural seam emerges
validation: Defer per Complexity axis; carry-in from prior S2-M1

title: No direct unit test for _per_project_disk_usage (per-project disk breakdown)
evidence: grep: no test references _per_project_disk_usage; dir_usage + info Backups section are tested
impact: The --by-project/disk output path is exercised only end-to-end, not unit-verified for correct per-project bytes/counts
recommended_action: Add a small test with a temp DB + fake session-diff files asserting rows/bytes

title: No direct unit test for confirm_destructive / _project_for_cwd
evidence: confirm_destructive behavior covered indirectly via cli_clean_backups cancel/dry-run tests; _project_for_cwd untested
impact: Seam and cwd->project mapping lack focused tests; low risk (simple, fail-soft)
recommended_action: Optionally add focused unit tests for confirm_destructive (yes/dry_run/assume_yes/EOF) and _project_for_cwd

title: Bare pytest can resolve an installed ocman instead of local source
evidence: README documents PYTHONPATH=. ; CI sets PYTHONPATH=.; local env has editable install (mitigated)
impact: Fresh clone with non-editable install could test wrong package
recommended_action: Already documented; optional conftest/pytest.ini to pin rootdir
validation: Carry-in; keep documented, optional pytest.ini in S7

title: README config template documents a nonexistent key default_model (real: default_compaction_model, default \\")"
impact: A user copying the template sets a dead key and misbelieves a default model is configured
validation: diff README template keys vs DEFAULT_CONFIG

title: README Argument Reference omits ~13 real flags
impact: Reader relying on the table cannot discover session-browsing/version flags

title: ARCHITECTURE understates recognized commands; README preprocessing list omits disk/du + delete project
evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:20 'info/help/ui/gui' only; preprocess_argv also rewrites disk/du, delete project, list*, show logs (ocman.py:3948-4033)
impact: Reader may think only 4 commands exist

title: ARCHITECTURE ocman_tui/ layout omits shipped css/ subdir
evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:21-23 lists app.py/core.py/widgets/ but not css/

title: README does not state the core value proposition (actually reclaims DB+filesystem space via VACUUM+file delete)
evidence: Reclaim verified: db_run_cleanup/delete run VACUUM (ocman.py:5031-5047) + delete session-diff files + report db/file space saved; competitor ocgc reportedly leaves ~95% behind (user-stated intent)
impact: New users/stakeholders don't see why ocman beats naive cleanup; headline benefit is buried
recommended_action: Add a concise, truthful 'actually reclaims space' point (VACUUM + file deletion + reported savings); do NOT name/denigrate a competitor in shipped docs unless user confirms

title: Cold-start orientation adequate; decision rationale is thin but improving
impact: A no-context engineer can orient; decision 'why' is spread across IPDs not a single log
recommended_action: No new file needed (respect existing convention); optionally note the IPD dir as the decisions home
